questions.prompt: What belongs in the daily check vs the weekly session?

choices.text: Daily is monitor-only: glance at 3-day-old spend/CPA/ROAS/hook rate/frequency, note anomalies, no edits. Weekly is action: 60-90 minute session covering creative refresh (3-5 new ads, 2-3 paused), performance review, hygiene, budget decisions, planning. Doing weekly work daily resets learning and over-reacts to noise. Doing daily work weekly misses fires.

questions.explanation: Daily = monitor; weekly = action. Don't conflate them.

questions.prompt: Account has been running 8 months. Daily ops only, no monthly or quarterly review. Performance slowly worse. Why?

choices.text: Skipped higher-order rhythms. Monthly audit catches stale CRM uploads, lookalike seed decay, audience overlap drift, AEM event-priority issues, retired campaigns lingering — each adds 2-5% efficiency loss compounding to 20-30% over months. Quarterly lift tests ground attribution; without them you're scaling on inflated numbers. Add a 2-3 hour monthly audit and an annual lift test; most of the recoverable performance is hidden in skipped hygiene.

questions.explanation: Skipped monthly + quarterly = silent decay. Add the higher rhythms.
